mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings and lifts equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth signals lose predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) specifically during late-cycle expansion periods in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 3.0 percent impose high borrowing costs that compress profit margins and trigger rapid capital outflows from equities over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and unemployment rate changes reveals labor market slack that suppresses wage inflation expectations and reduces equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction term shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity that directly translates into positive equity market momentum over the following month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns (directional accuracy < 50%) across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105 on the 2017 base index signals severe supply chain constraints or demand collapse that triggers immediate sell-offs in equity markets over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations when INDPRO drops below 105.
